prep.summary: Review your recent work notes to identify persistent operational friction—specifically stalling pilots, environment constraints for the 2026 P&C rollouts, and unstable AI prompt pipelines. Address critical technical decisions—including the ServiceNow vendor lock-in risks and AWS/Iceberg infrastructure transitions. Secure updates from Mark and Deepak on these cross-cutting hurdles—and prioritize the upcoming talent calibrations.
tasks: Ask Mark for the timeline—and specific criteria—for upcoming team calibrations.
Resolve outstanding technical decisions—specifically ServiceNow vendor lock-in versus internal event monitoring.
Align with Deepak to unblock the stalling Resiliency/NART pilots—focusing on the missing PO3 process mapping.
Establish a sustainable framework for prompt governance—replacing the bespoke templates currently managed by Mark and Adam.
prep.title: Bi-weekly - 1:1 w/Mark Francis | Deepak

events.summary: EMG Engage - Intro to Architecture w/Mark Francis | J

prep.summary: Review the recent Claims Reqs meetings from May 18—where you and Adam developed a bespoke, non-sustainable prompt process to handle the influx of requirements from Sarah's team. You must align these overlapping AI initiatives with Andy Napalese's "Foundry" framework—a P&C-specific product development flywheel—to transition from requirements clarity to Jira. Address the data quality gaps immediately—specifically by leveraging Jonathan's Snowflake warehouse access to automate validation before change records hit human review.
tasks: Transition the non-sustainable prompt pipeline developed by Mark and Adam into a structured feedback mechanism—like a spreadsheet—to flag hallucinations and regulatory gaps.
Align parallel P&C AI initiatives—specifically Genie and the projects run by Shane London and Chase Secula—with the Foundry framework.
Automate data validation by implementing a batch job that flags incomplete or low-quality change records prior to Change Review Group sessions.
Utilize Jonathan's Snowflake warehouse access to ingest and analyze P&C change records against your architecture checklist.
prep.title: Sync with the P&C IAs

events.summary: EMG Engage - Intro to Architecture w/Mark Francis | S...

prep.summary: You must address the paused CMT/Telematics initiative—the core focus of your last discussion with Vince. Focus your preparation on compiling the preliminary forensics and impact attestations—both are critical blockers. Resolve the outstanding questions regarding member remediation credits—and prepare for the upcoming integration of speeding into driver scoring.
tasks: Compile the preliminary forensics and impact attestations for the paused CMT/Telematics project.
Formulate the strategy for potential member remediation credits.
Integrate speeding metrics into the upcoming driver scoring iteration.
Establish the steady-state framework for loading 2024 and 2025 telematics data.
prep.title: Sync with Mark | Vincent Sanchez (Follow-up)

events.summary: Review SN POC Closeout for Dan G - Session 1

prep.summary: You must prepare for the ServiceNow Proof of Concept—SN POC—closeout review with Dan Griffiths using the decisions from the May 28 alignment session. The team plans to scale back the presentation deck—specifically hiding slides 6, 7, 15, and 515—to focus on a highly concise, story-driven narrative rather than a simplistic "happy path" ACH flow. You need to address critical concerns regarding platform value—namely vendor lock-in and pricing—while actively aligning with the Experience Intelligence team to prevent siloed communication.
tasks: Revise the presentation deck to transition directly from slide 5 to slide 9—removing unnecessary technical clutter.
Align with the Experience Intelligence team to deliver a unified message to leadership regarding process monitoring.
Evaluate the long-term cost of ServiceNow against homegrown solutions—factoring in potential vendor lock-in and discounted pricing structures.
Define the broader technical complexities of the eventing architecture—moving past the limited scope of the initial ACH flow.
prep.title: Review SN POC Closeout for Dan G - Session 1

events.summary: WOR Submissions, due by EOD Every Weds

Focus · Next up
8:30 AM — 9:00 AM

Bi-weekly - 1:1 w/Mark Francis | Deepak

Prep

Review your recent work notes to identify persistent operational friction—specifically stalling pilots, environment constraints for the 2026 P&C rollouts, and unstable AI prompt pipelines. Address critical technical decisions—including the ServiceNow vendor lock-in risks and AWS/Iceberg infrastructure transitions. Secure updates from Mark and Deepak on these cross-cutting hurdles—and prioritize the upcoming talent calibrations.

  • Ask Mark for the timeline—and specific criteria—for upcoming team calibrations.
  • Resolve outstanding technical decisions—specifically ServiceNow vendor lock-in versus internal event monitoring.
  • Align with Deepak to unblock the stalling Resiliency/NART pilots—focusing on the missing PO3 process mapping.
  • Establish a sustainable framework for prompt governance—replacing the bespoke templates currently managed by Mark and Adam.
